channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Michigan governor threatens to pull troops from D.C. if used for Trump task force
item.description: Michigan Gov. Whitmer is one of four Democrats who sent their states' National Guard troops to Washington, D.C. ahead of America 250 celebrations in recent weeks, amid President Trump's ongoing — and controversial — deployment in the city.
item.content:encoded:
Michigan Gov. Whitmer is one of four Democrats who sent their states' National Guard troops to Washington, D.C. ahead of America 250 celebrations in recent weeks, amid President Trump's ongoing — and controversial — deployment in the city.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Wheelchair users say private equity is making repairs harder
item.description: People who rely on wheelchairs say that industry consolidation driven by private equity means long delays in getting them fixed, which isolates them from society and endangers their health.
item.content:encoded: People who rely on wheelchairs say that industry consolidation driven by private equity means long delays in getting them fixed, which isolates them from society and endangers their health.
item.dc:creator: Meghan H Smith

item.title: When resources run thin, volunteer ranchers fight local fires
item.description: Rangeland Fire Protection Districts are volunteer groups made up of neighbors who protect their property and nearby land from wildfires, especially when federal and state resources are stretched thin.
item.content:encoded: Rangeland Fire Protection Districts are volunteer groups made up of neighbors who protect their property and nearby land from wildfires, especially when federal and state resources are stretched thin.
item.dc:creator: Alejandro Figueroa

item.title: The Temporary Protected Status program may effectively be over. Here's what we know.
item.description: A Supreme Court ruling gives the Trump administration space to strip this status from hundreds of thousands of more people from the few remaining countries with this program.
item.content:encoded:
A Supreme Court ruling gives the Trump administration space to strip this status from hundreds of thousands of more people from the few remaining countries with this program.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Mother of murdered sailor demands Navy change how it handles sexual assault cases
item.description: The mother of a murdered sailor is demanding the Navy to make long-term changes after her daughter died at the hands of a shipmate.
item.content:encoded: The mother of a murdered sailor is demanding the Navy to make long-term changes after her daughter died at the hands of a shipmate.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Supreme Court takes sledgehammer to much of federal government's regulatory structure
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down most of the limits that Congress and the courts had previously established to protect the independence of regulatory agencies that comprise much of the federal government.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court struck down most of the limits that Congress and the courts had previously established to protect the independence of regulatory agencies that comprise much of the federal government.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Supreme Court restricts use of geofence warrants
item.description: Writing for the 6-3 majority, Justice Elena Kagan said that the technique, known as geofencing, violates the Fourth Amendment's prohibition against unreasonable searches.
item.content:encoded:
Writing for the 6-3 majority, Justice Elena Kagan said that the technique, known as geofencing, violates the Fourth Amendment's prohibition against unreasonable searches.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Supreme Court says Fed's Lisa Cook can stay in her job for now
item.description: The court's decision means Cook can stay in her position as her challenge to her dismissal plays out in the lower courts.
item.content:encoded:
The court's decision means Cook can stay in her position as her challenge to her dismissal plays out in the lower courts.

(Image credit: Drew Angerer)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
item.description: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.
item.content:encoded:
In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: The Supreme Court upholds grace periods for mail-in ballots, siding against the GOP
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has upheld a Mississippi law that allows election officials to count mail-in ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but received up to five days after it.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Supreme Court has upheld a Mississippi law that allows election officials to count mail-in ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but received up to five days after it.

(Image credit: Rebecca Droke)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez
